Event: He and his ex-wife remain on friendly terms after divorce
Explanation: Maintaining a warm post-divorce relationship benefits both of them socially and emotionally.

Event: They dance together at a wedding
Explanation: Choosing to dance together shows mutual comfort and gives both a positive social moment.

Event: Jay grows more comfortable around Manny, Cam, and Phil over time
Explanation: His emotional growth improves his own relationships and their comfort with him.

Event: Jay will put himself through pain, misery or loss before admitting he might be wrong
Explanation: His stubbornness harms him and strains others with no real compensating gain.

Event: He is a very good stepfather to Manny and loves him dearly
Explanation: Investing in Manny’s wellbeing strengthens their bond and Manny’s development.

Event: He loves Gloria deeply and considers her son his own
Explanation: Emotional commitment enriches his own life and provides security and love to Gloria and Manny.

Event: He dotes on Stella and keeps a butler dog mannequin he finds hilarious
Explanation: Caring for a pet and expressing quirky humor brings him joy and lightens the household.

Event: He confides things to Cam that he won’t tell anyone else
Explanation: Trusting Cam gives him support and deepens Cam’s sense of importance and connection.

Event: He absolutely dotes on his dog and takes an interest in dog shows
Explanation: Active care and hobby engagement give him satisfaction and provide attentive care and interest around dogs.

Event: He always means well and will do what’s right in the end
Explanation: Choosing the right action ultimately helps others and gives him moral and relational benefits.

Event: Jay’s mother runs onto the field and yells at his coach to defend him
Explanation: She protects and comforts her son yet undermines the coach and the structure of the game.

Event: Jay’s homophobia decreases slightly over time
Explanation: Reducing prejudice eases his own conflicts and makes life easier for his gay family members.

Event: He throws a lavish wedding for Cam and Mitch and calls them his sons
Explanation: Lavish support affirms his sons, improving their happiness and his connection to them.

Event: He fusses over their crying adoptive daughter, who stops crying
Explanation: Comforting his granddaughter soothes her and likely gives him emotional satisfaction.

Event: He constantly gives Phil a hard time for not being “manly” enough
Explanation: He reinforces his own identity and amusement at the expense of Phil’s feelings and comfort.

Event: Phil turns the other cheek to Jay’s teasing
Explanation: Phil protects the relationship by absorbing hurt, benefiting Jay while emotionally costing himself.

Event: Jay does not receive emotional support from his father growing up
Explanation: The father avoids emotional investment while significantly harming Jay’s emotional development.

Event: Jay’s father calls him “Becky” when kissing him goodnight
Explanation: The father amuses himself or is careless while undermining Jay’s sense of being seen and respected.

Event: Jay’s father gives the promised fishing cabin to Becky instead
Explanation: He favors another child or gains convenience while deeply betraying Jay’s trust.

Event: Jay’s father makes him a sandwich and tells him to forget the girl after Jay is stood up
Explanation: He provides comfort and practical advice that helps Jay cope with rejection.

Event: Jay’s father throws him into the lake to get him over fear of swimming
Explanation: The harsh method risks physical/psychological harm and only dubiously helps with the fear.

Event: Jay’s father refuses to grant him a start-up loan
Explanation: He protects his own resources while blocking Jay’s opportunity, contributing to Jay’s resentment.

Event: Jay tries hard to do better parenting Manny and Joe after recognizing past mistakes
Explanation: Self-reflection and improved parenting benefit him emotionally and greatly help the younger kids.
